title: eupatorium symphyopappus var. pohlianum
intro: Eupatorium symphyopappus var. pohlianum, also known as White Snakeroot, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family native to North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote wound healing, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Eupatorium symphyopappus var. pohlianum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
